text: As you can see the patient presented with pain abdomen on the right side and there is a evident serous ovarian cyst on the right side measuring approximately ten to twelve centimeters. Cystectomy is a procedure which needs to be performed extremely meticulously especially for young women who need to preserve their fertility.

The surgeon gently dissects the ovarian cortex to separate and strip the ovarian cyst from the surrounding tissue, maintaining a bloodless field. Gentle traction and counter traction are applied to facilitate the dissection. The option to rupture the cyst for debulking is considered to ease the continuation of the cystectomy.

The surgeon performs a ribbonization technique by cutting open the ovarian cyst wall into a long ribbon shape. Using a trocar for traction and scissors for cutting, the cyst wall is extracted from the peritoneal cavity through two ports.
